title: Verify wishlist backend ownership and persistence
verify: Real Medusa/PostgreSQL flow persists favorite across fresh process query and removes it through Store API.
Two authenticated customers remain isolated for list/add/remove under product ID reuse.
Duplicate/concurrent add, repeated remove, guest denial, malformed input, exact item shape, and backend failure match contract.
Missing, unpublished, channel-invisible, and inactive-category add use the same 404 and list omission; out-of-stock remains visible/unavailable.
evidence_required: Real Medusa/PostgreSQL persistence/restart output using synthetic identifiers/content where useful.
Two-customer ownership and concurrency assertions without real PII or credential values.
Exact projection, unified hidden 404, visibility restoration, and out-of-stock assertions.
HUMAN_CHECKPOINT and rollback/recovery evidence required by T3 policy.
constraints: Exercise real Medusa module/routes/local PostgreSQL and synthetic customers/products.
Synthetic product names/IDs and wishlist contents may appear in evidence when needed; real PII, production data, cookies, bearer values, OAuth tokens, session IDs, and secrets may not.
Use supported product publication/sales-channel/category/stock changes and restore fixtures.
Do not modify production wishlist behavior to make acceptance pass.
invariants: One durable row per customer/product under concurrency.
All hidden visibility causes are non-observable through the same 404.
Product lifecycle visibility never deletes or corrupts other favorites; out-of-stock remains visible.
purpose: Prove wishlist persistence and authorization against real backend boundaries before browser acceptance.
success_outcome: Backend acceptance demonstrates durable, customer-isolated, idempotent wishlist behavior with correct product lifecycle projection.
anti_goals: Do not use real PII, production data, credentials, tokens, or session identifiers.
Do not replace real PostgreSQL/module/API boundaries with mocks.
Do not add new wishlist behavior.

stop_conditions: Acceptance cannot exercise real Wishlist Module/PostgreSQL/Store routes.
Tests require real PII, production data, live provider credentials, cookies, bearer values, OAuth tokens, session IDs, or secrets in evidence.
A discovered implementation defect requires edits outside acceptance scope.
